item.title: A peek inside the 'Black Box' - interpreting neural networks
item.description: Motivation Propelled by advancements in modern computer technology, deep learning has re-emerged as perhaps the most promising artificial intelligence (AI) technology of the last two decades. By treating problems as a nested, hierarchy of hidden layers deep artificial neural networks achieve the power and flexibility necessary for AI systems to navigate complex real-world environments. Unfortunately, their very nature has earned them a reputation as Black Box algorithms and their lack of interpretability remains a major impediment to their more wide-spread application.

item.title: Resume
item.description: Last updated on 2021-02-01. For up-to-date information see also my LinkedIn profile. Contact Info altmeyerpat@gmail.com GitHub Patrick Altmeyer Currently searching for a PhD student position I am currently looking for PhD opportunities to perform cutting-edge research on the intersection of explainable AI, financial markets and economics. Education Barcelona Graduate School of Economics MSc Data Science Barcelona, Spain 2021 Thesis: - Barcelona Graduate School of Economics MSc Finance

item.title: Welcome
item.description: Welcome to my website! Having worked with R Markdown and some of Yihui Xie’s amazing packages for years, I have only now come across his amazing blogdown package. For a while I have been thinking about a good way to share some of my work and actually started collecting snippets in a Gitbook through bookdown quite some time ago. While the book is a work-in-progress that I aim to finish at eventually, this blog is where I aim to regularly share content related to my work as a data scientist and economist.

item.title: About
item.description: Economist with a knack for computer, data and decision science. Curious about hybrid intelligence, responsible AI, Bayesian statistics and behavioural and life sciences.
